title: Preserve Agent Plugin Lifecycle Dependency Direction
forbids: root service capability declarations or service-level models depending upward on sealed capability modules, any lifecycle service implementation or package entrypoint depending on an outward binding, provider code reaching current-main through governance module imports instead of the neutral root dependency, CLI access to lifecycle binding facades, module router bypass of module context through root dependency or provided-resource access, root router bypass of impl.router and module routers, service imports of concrete providers or controller implementation, module-port re-exports from the retired internal bucket, local provider-binding imports outside the provider composition root, raw artifact-provider imports outside the one lifecycle client composition root, any CLI-local output binding or release artifact/evidence projection, pure release values outside command parsing, caller-owned releases, packaging, or provider artifact/evidence semantics, a second production value consumer of the lifecycle service client, and any peer service client reachable from the lifecycle projection
why: Root capability declarations remain independent of sealed governance, packaging, providers, releases, and vendors implementations, and that sealed set grows monotonically as context-direction slices land. Governance owns current-main selection while one closed service-composition middleware derives its neutral observation reader from the raw content-workspace capability. The CLI selects exactly one raw artifact repository provider and explicit root for lifecycle service context; lifecycle middleware privately derives artifact and evidence semantics from that pair, and each consuming module projects only its required capability from the same provided context. Routers consume only their local module context. The service owns lifecycle semantics behind module-local router handlers and derives provider-domain repositories under module middleware. The root composes module routers through impl.router. Runtime construction narrows through one exact CLI composition root. Native contracts flow directly from their resource owner, and the CLI-local native binding enters only at the provider composition root. Pure release algebra enters command parsing through the release surface; no lifecycle binding facade remains.
remediate: Declare root capabilities and cross-module observation contracts under service/model, derive current-main once in service/middleware/current-main.ts from the raw content-workspace capability and governance-owned selector, select the raw artifact repository and root once at the CLI composition root, derive lifecycle artifact and evidence semantics in service/middleware/artifacts.ts through private service repositories, project read-only artifact capability from service-provided context in each consuming module.ts, consume only local module context in routers, derive provider repositories and adapters under module provided context, project exact capabilities into each module from module.ts, implement procedure handlers from the local module surface, compose module routers through impl.router, keep remaining ports protocol/type-only, construct the lifecycle client and raw artifact provider only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/client.ts, import native contracts directly from their resource owner, consume the CLI-local provider binding only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/providers/node-runtime.ts, delete lifecycle binding facades, and import pure release parsers directly from the release surface in commands/input.ts.
message: Lifecycle dependencies flow from root-owned capabilities and neutral cross-module observations into sealed module contexts and handlers, then through impl.router and exact CLI composition roots; pure release algebra does not flow through host bindings.
